walkthrough.title: Download and Launch the App
walkthrough.text: Before anything else, you need the Descent: Legends of the Dark companion app. Download it on a tablet, phone, or computer. The app drives the entire game — it manages enemies, reveals the map, tracks your quest progress, and tells stories. Launch it now and create a new campaign. The app will guide you through a lot of the setup, but we will walk through the physical components together so you know what everything is.

walkthrough.title: Unpack the 3D Terrain
walkthrough.text: Descent comes with a huge collection of 3D terrain pieces. You have got staircases, bookshelves, barricades, trees, barrels, and elevation platforms. Do not try to assemble the entire map right now. The app will tell you which terrain tiles and 3D pieces to place as you explore. For now, sort the terrain pieces into groups so you can find them quickly when the app calls for them. The large double-sided map tiles go in one stack, and the 3D furniture and terrain go in another area within easy reach.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Heroes
walkthrough.text: Each player selects one hero. There are six heroes in the box, each with a unique playstyle. Brynn is a tough human guardian. Galaden is an elven ranger with ranged attacks. Chance is a hyrrinx rogue who excels at stealth. Kehli is a dwarven engineer who builds devices. Syrus is a human elementalist who wields magic. Vaerix is a hybrid sentinel who can tank and support. With three players, each person picks one hero. Read the hero card descriptions and choose whoever sounds most fun to you.

walkthrough.title: Collect Your Hero Materials
walkthrough.text: Once everyone has chosen a hero, gather that hero's components. Each hero gets a hero card showing their stats, a detailed plastic miniature, and a set of custom hero dice matching their color. Place your hero card in front of you with the miniature nearby. The hero card shows your health, stamina, and defense values. Keep the dice within reach — you will be rolling them every time you attack or defend.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Starting Weapons
walkthrough.text: Each hero begins with two starting weapon cards. The app will tell you which weapons your hero starts with. These weapon cards are double-sided — each side represents a different combat mode. For example, a sword might have a quick slash on one side and a heavy cleave on the other. Place both weapon cards next to your hero card with one side face up. You can flip them during the game to switch modes. We will cover how flipping works during rules.

walkthrough.title: Collect Starting Skill Cards
walkthrough.text: Each hero also starts with a small set of skill cards unique to their class. The app will specify which skills you begin with. Skill cards give you special abilities you can use during your turn, like defensive stances, healing, or powerful attacks. Place your skill cards face up next to your weapons. As the campaign progresses, you will earn new skills and upgrade existing ones.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Damage and Fatigue Tokens
walkthrough.text: Find the damage tokens and fatigue tokens. Damage tokens are placed on your hero card when you take hits — when your damage equals your health, you are knocked out. Fatigue tokens represent overexertion. You gain fatigue when you push your limits using certain abilities. Place both token pools in a shared area where everyone can reach them. You will be using these constantly throughout every quest.

walkthrough.title: Sort the Condition Tokens
walkthrough.text: Descent uses several condition tokens that can affect heroes and enemies. The main ones are poison, stun, disease, terrified, and burning. Sort these into separate piles near the damage and fatigue tokens. When the app or a card effect applies a condition, you place the matching token on the affected figure's card. Each condition has a specific negative effect that we will cover in the rules section.

walkthrough.title: Organize the Enemy Figures
walkthrough.text: The box contains a variety of enemy miniatures — bandits, undead, monsters, and more. You do not need to sort all of them right now. The app will tell you exactly which enemy figures to place and where during the quest. Just make sure the enemy figures are accessible and you can identify which type is which. Each enemy type has a matching enemy card that the app manages digitally.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Map Tiles
walkthrough.text: The app will now show you the starting map layout for your first quest. Place the indicated map tiles on the table and add any 3D terrain pieces the app requests. The map starts small — you will only see the initial area. As heroes explore, the app will reveal new tiles and terrain. This fog of war system means you never know exactly what lies ahead. Leave space on the table for the map to expand in any direction.

walkthrough.title: Place Hero Miniatures
walkthrough.text: The app will show you where to place your hero miniatures on the starting map tiles. Each hero occupies one space on the map. Place your miniatures on the indicated spaces. Your heroes start together and will spread out as the quest begins. Make sure everyone can see the map clearly — you will be making tactical movement decisions from the very first turn.

walkthrough.title: Review the App Interface
walkthrough.text: Take a moment to familiarize yourself with the app interface. The main screen shows the map with clickable tokens for interactions. The hero panel lets you view each hero's current weapons, skills, and status. The log tracks the story narrative and any instructions. The threat bar at the top shows how much time pressure you are under — more on that in the rules. Everyone at the table should be able to see the app screen, so position it centrally or use a tablet propped up where all players can read it.

walkthrough.title: Understand Campaign Persistence
walkthrough.text: One important thing before you start playing — Descent is a campaign game spanning sixteen quests. Your heroes carry their progress between quests. Weapons you craft, skills you learn, and items you find all persist. The app saves your campaign automatically. Between quests, you will visit a hub town where you can craft new gear and upgrade your heroes. This first quest is your introduction, so do not worry about long-term strategy yet — just learn the basics and have fun.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Begin
walkthrough.text: Setup is complete. You have your heroes chosen, weapons and skills laid out, tokens organized, and the starting map assembled. The app is running and ready to guide you through your first quest. Remember, this is a fully cooperative game — you are all working together against the app-controlled enemies. Talk to each other, plan your moves, and support one another. Let's move on to learning the rules so you know what to do on your turn.

walkthrough.title: Game Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Each quest in Descent is played in a series of rounds. Every round has two phases. First is the Hero Phase, where each hero takes a full turn performing actions. Second is the Darkness Phase, where the app activates enemies, advances the threat timer, and triggers story events. The round then repeats — heroes act, then darkness responds. This continues until the quest objective is completed or the heroes are defeated.

walkthrough.title: The Hero Phase
walkthrough.text: During the Hero Phase, each hero takes one complete turn. The order in which heroes take their turns is entirely up to the group — there is no fixed turn order. Talk with your teammates about who should go first based on the tactical situation. Each hero gets three actions on their turn. You can take the same action multiple times if you want, with one exception — you cannot attack the same enemy twice in one turn.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move
walkthrough.text: The Move action lets you travel across the map. When you move, you get a number of movement points based on your hero's speed stat. Each space you enter costs one movement point. Moving into a space with 3D terrain on it may cost additional movement points — the app will indicate this. You can split your movement around other actions. For example, you could move two spaces, attack, then move one more space. Diagonal movement is not allowed — you move orthogonally through connected spaces.

walkthrough.title: Action: Attack
walkthrough.text: To attack an enemy, you must be in range. Melee weapons require you to be adjacent to the target. Ranged weapons can hit targets several spaces away as indicated on the weapon card. When you attack, roll your hero's dice pool. The dice show hits, surges, and sometimes blanks. Count up your hits. Then check for any surge abilities on your weapon — surges can add bonus damage, apply conditions, or trigger special effects. Report your results to the app, and it calculates the damage dealt to the enemy after its defense.

walkthrough.title: Understanding Dice
walkthrough.text: Each hero has their own custom dice that match their playstyle. Dice faces show different symbols. A hit symbol deals one point of damage. A surge symbol can be spent on your weapon's surge abilities — these are the special effects listed on the weapon card. Some faces show both a hit and a surge together. Blank faces are misses. The number and type of dice you roll depends on your weapon card. Getting comfortable with your dice is key to understanding your combat potential.

walkthrough.title: Surge Abilities
walkthrough.text: Surge abilities are the special effects printed on your weapon cards. After rolling, you can spend surge symbols to activate these abilities. Each surge ability has a cost — usually one or two surge symbols. Common surge effects include dealing extra damage, piercing through enemy armor, applying conditions like stun or poison, or healing yourself or an ally. You can activate multiple surge abilities on a single attack as long as you have enough surge symbols to pay for them. Surges you do not spend are simply lost.

walkthrough.title: Weapon Flipping
walkthrough.text: This is one of the most important mechanics in Descent. Every weapon card has two sides, and each side offers different stats, surge abilities, and sometimes even different attack types. You can flip a weapon card to its other side by using the Ready Weapon action or when certain abilities allow it. The side facing up is your active weapon mode. For example, a staff might have a single-target fire blast on one side and an area-of-effect frost wave on the other. Knowing when to flip your weapons is crucial to adapting to different combat situations.

walkthrough.title: Action: Ready Weapon
walkthrough.text: The Ready Weapon action lets you flip one or both of your weapon cards to their other side. This is how you switch combat modes. If your current weapon side is not working against the enemies you are facing, spend an action to flip. You can also flip weapons during a rest or when certain skill cards allow it. Planning when to flip is important — you do not want to waste an action flipping mid-combat if you could have prepared earlier. Think about what enemies you expect to face and ready your weapons accordingly.

walkthrough.title: Action: Interact
walkthrough.text: The Interact action is used when you are adjacent to or on a space with an interaction token shown by the app. These tokens represent doors, chests, levers, NPCs, quest objectives, and other interactive elements. When you interact, tap the token in the app and it will tell you what happens — maybe you open a locked door, find loot, trigger a story event, or advance the quest objective. Interactions are how you progress through the story and discover new areas of the map.

walkthrough.title: Action: Explore
walkthrough.text: When you move to the edge of the revealed map, you may be able to explore further. The Explore action tells the app you are pushing into unknown territory. The app then reveals new map tiles and 3D terrain, placing them adjacent to where you explored. New enemies may also appear on the freshly revealed tiles. Exploration is essential for progressing through quests, but be careful — rushing ahead alone can leave you surrounded by enemies with no allies nearby to help.

walkthrough.title: Action: Use Skill
walkthrough.text: Skill cards give your hero special abilities beyond basic attacks and movement. Each skill card describes when and how it can be used. Some skills cost an action to activate, while others are free reactions that trigger in response to specific events. When you use a skill that costs an action, flip or exhaust the skill card to show it has been used. Some skills recover automatically at the start of your next turn, while others require a specific action or condition to refresh. Your hero's unique skills define their role on the team.

walkthrough.title: The Fatigue System
walkthrough.text: Fatigue is a core mechanic that adds risk-reward tension to every decision. Some powerful abilities require you to suffer fatigue to use them. You can also choose to suffer fatigue to gain extra movement points or perform heroic actions. When you gain fatigue, place a fatigue token on your hero card. Here is the catch — fatigue tokens fill up your hero card alongside damage. If your combined damage and fatigue ever reach your health total, you are knocked out. Fatigue essentially shrinks your effective health pool. Use it wisely.

walkthrough.title: Managing Fatigue
walkthrough.text: You can remove fatigue by using certain skills or by resting. Some heroes have abilities that convert fatigue into other benefits. The key is to push your limits when it matters most — an extra move to reach a quest objective or a powerful ability to finish off a dangerous enemy — but not to exhaust yourself recklessly. Going into the Darkness Phase with high fatigue is dangerous because enemy attacks deal damage, and if damage plus fatigue hits your health limit, you go down.

walkthrough.title: Defense and Taking Damage
walkthrough.text: When an enemy attacks your hero, the app tells you how much damage is incoming. You then roll your defense dice. Each shield symbol on the dice blocks one point of damage. Any unblocked damage is applied as damage tokens on your hero card. Some armor and skill cards provide additional defense bonuses. If your total damage plus fatigue equals or exceeds your health, your hero is knocked out. Knocked-out heroes are not eliminated — an ally can revive you on their turn — but being down costs the team precious time.

walkthrough.title: Knocked Out and Revival
walkthrough.text: When a hero is knocked out, they fall in their current space. They cannot take actions until revived. Any hero adjacent to a knocked-out hero can spend an action to revive them. The revived hero recovers some health as indicated by the app and removes all fatigue. Being knocked out is a significant setback — not only does the downed hero lose their turn, but another hero must spend one of their three precious actions to revive them. Avoid getting knocked out if at all possible.

walkthrough.title: The Darkness Phase
walkthrough.text: After all heroes have completed their turns, the Darkness Phase begins. Tap the Darkness Phase button in the app. The app now takes control. It activates each enemy group on the map, telling you exactly how they move and who they attack. It resolves enemy attacks and applies damage to targeted heroes. The app also advances the threat timer and may trigger story events, spawn new enemies, or change the map. You have no actions during this phase — you simply follow the app's instructions.

walkthrough.title: Enemy Activation
walkthrough.text: During the Darkness Phase, the app activates enemies one group at a time. It tells you how each enemy moves — toward which hero, how many spaces — and then who it attacks and how much damage it deals. Different enemy types have different behaviors. Some rush toward the nearest hero, others target the most wounded hero, and some use ranged attacks from a distance. Pay attention to enemy behavior patterns so you can predict and plan around them during the Hero Phase.

walkthrough.title: The Threat Timer
walkthrough.text: The threat bar in the app is a countdown clock that creates urgency. It advances each round during the Darkness Phase. As it increases, bad things happen — stronger enemies spawn, environmental hazards appear, and the difficulty ramps up. If the threat reaches its maximum, the quest may become significantly harder or even end in failure. The threat timer is why you cannot just turtle and play safe forever. You need to balance fighting enemies with pushing toward the quest objective before time runs out.

walkthrough.title: Elevation and 3D Terrain
walkthrough.text: The 3D terrain pieces are not just decorative — they affect gameplay. Elevation matters in Descent. Figures on higher ground gain combat advantages. Stairs and ladders let you move between elevation levels, sometimes costing extra movement points. Some terrain blocks line of sight for ranged attacks, while other terrain provides cover. Barricades and obstacles may block movement entirely. Always pay attention to the vertical dimension of the map — high ground is tactically valuable.

walkthrough.title: Exploration Tokens
walkthrough.text: As you explore the map, the app places various tokens on spaces. Search tokens let you find items and equipment. Interaction tokens mark quest objectives, NPCs, and story elements. Some tokens are hidden until you get close. Always be on the lookout for these tokens — they often contain critical items, healing potions, or information needed to complete the quest. Missing exploration tokens means missing potential upgrades and advantages.

walkthrough.title: Conditions
walkthrough.text: Several conditions can affect heroes and enemies during combat. Poison deals damage over time at the start of the affected figure's activation. Stun causes the figure to lose an action on their next turn. Disease reduces the effectiveness of healing. Terrified limits movement options. Burning deals fire damage each round. Conditions are applied through enemy attacks, terrain hazards, or ability effects. Removing conditions usually requires specific skills or spending actions. Conditions stack up fast if you are not careful, so address them before they spiral.

walkthrough.title: Quest Objectives
walkthrough.text: Every quest has specific objectives that the app tracks. Objectives might include defeating a boss enemy, rescuing NPCs, collecting items, reaching a location, or making a story choice. The app clearly displays your current objective and updates it as you progress. Some quests have branching objectives — your choices affect the story and may change what happens in future quests. Focus on the objective and work as a team to accomplish it before the threat timer overwhelms you.

walkthrough.title: Crafting Between Quests
walkthrough.text: Between quests, you return to the hub town. Here the app lets you craft new weapons and armor using materials you have collected during your quests. Crafting is how you upgrade your heroes over the campaign. New weapons unlock new combat modes and stronger surge abilities. You can also purchase skill upgrades and consumable items. Crafting decisions are shared by the group — discuss who needs upgrades most and how to allocate your limited resources.

walkthrough.title: Campaign Progression
walkthrough.text: The sixteen-quest campaign tells a branching narrative. Your choices during quests affect the story — which quests you unlock, which allies you gain, and how the final confrontation unfolds. Hero progression carries forward, so a hero who collects strong weapons early becomes more powerful in later quests. The difficulty also scales as the campaign progresses, introducing tougher enemies and more complex map layouts. Every quest matters, and every decision has consequences.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: You win a quest by completing its objective before the threat timer runs out or before all heroes are defeated. If all heroes are knocked out simultaneously, or if the threat reaches critical levels, the quest may end in failure. Failed quests do not end the campaign — the story adapts and continues, though you may face harder conditions going forward. Success rewards you with better loot, more crafting materials, and a stronger narrative outcome. Work together, play smart, and push forward — that is how you conquer the darkness.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Quest
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first quest in Descent: Legends of the Dark. In this practice tutorial, we will walk through several rounds of play with three heroes — Brynn the guardian, Galaden the ranger, and Chance the rogue. We will demonstrate the core actions, show how the Darkness Phase works, and watch our team make some common mistakes so you can learn from them. Follow along with the moves even if you are playing different heroes. Ready? Let's descend into the darkness.

walkthrough.title: The Starting Map
walkthrough.text: The app has revealed the starting area — a crumbling dungeon entrance with two rooms connected by a narrow corridor. There are enemies visible in the second room, a search token near the entrance, and an exploration token at the far edge of the map. Our three heroes start together near the dungeon entrance. Remember, the map will expand as we explore.

walkthrough.title: Deciding Turn Order
walkthrough.text: Before anyone acts, the team discusses who should go first. This is a cooperative game, so there is no fixed turn order — you decide together. Brynn is the toughest hero with the best defense, so the team decides she should lead the way into the corridor. Galaden will go second to provide ranged support. Chance will go third to sneak around for flanking positions. Always discuss your plan before the first hero acts.

walkthrough.title: Brynn's Turn: Move and Explore
walkthrough.text: Brynn takes her first action — Move. She spends her movement points to advance through the corridor toward the second room. With her second action, she moves again to reach the edge of the revealed map. For her third action, she uses Explore. The app reveals a new section of the dungeon — a large chamber with elevated platforms and two goblin archers on the upper level. The app instructs us to place new map tiles, a staircase, and the goblin miniatures. The map just got bigger and more dangerous.

walkthrough.title: Why Brynn Led the Way
walkthrough.text: Having Brynn explore first was smart because she is the toughest hero. When new enemies appear from exploration, Brynn can absorb the first round of attacks with her high defense. If Chance had explored alone, those goblin archers could deal serious damage to the rogue's smaller health pool. Always let your tankiest hero be the one to push into unknown territory.

walkthrough.title: Galaden's Turn: Move and Attack
walkthrough.text: Galaden moves up behind Brynn with his first action. With his second action, he takes aim at one of the goblin archers on the elevated platform. His bow has the range to reach them from here. He rolls his dice and gets two hits and one surge. He spends the surge on his weapon's Pierce ability, which ignores one point of the goblin's armor. He reports the results to the app — the goblin takes solid damage. For his third action, Galaden moves to a position with cover behind a pillar.

walkthrough.title: Chance's Turn: The First Mistake
walkthrough.text: Chance sees the search token near the dungeon entrance and the exploration token at the edge of the new chamber. The search token is right there, just one move away. But instead of grabbing it, Chance rushes forward past Brynn to use all three actions on moving as deep into the new chamber as possible. Chance ends up far ahead of both allies, exposed to multiple enemies, and completely ignores the search token.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Do Not Skip Exploration Tokens
walkthrough.text: That search token Chance ignored could have contained a healing potion, a piece of equipment, or a crafting material. Exploration tokens are how you find critical items that make the difference between winning and losing a quest. Rushing past them means leaving potential upgrades on the table. Always have at least one hero sweep the area for tokens before the group pushes forward. Those items add up over the campaign and you cannot go back for them later.

walkthrough.title: The Darkness Phase Hits
walkthrough.text: All three heroes have taken their turns. Time for the Darkness Phase. We tap the button in the app. The goblins activate — one archer fires at Chance, who is exposed in the open with no allies nearby. Chance takes heavy damage. The other goblin moves toward Brynn and attacks, but Brynn's defense dice block most of the damage. The threat timer advances one step. If Chance had stayed closer to the group, the goblins would have had to split their attention.

walkthrough.title: Round 2: Regrouping
walkthrough.text: New round. The team needs to regroup. Brynn moves up the stairs to engage the goblins on the elevated platform — higher ground gives her better positioning. Galaden fires another ranged attack from his safe position. Chance, wounded and exposed, uses one action to move back toward allies and another to use a skill card that provides some healing. Good recovery — Chance is back in the fight.

walkthrough.title: Brynn's Elevated Attack
walkthrough.text: Brynn is now on the elevated platform with the goblins. She attacks the wounded goblin archer with her sword. She rolls three hits — enough to defeat it. The app removes the enemy and describes it crumbling. Having the high ground gave Brynn an advantage on this attack. Remember, elevation matters in Descent. Whenever possible, fight from above rather than below.

walkthrough.title: The Second Mistake: Fatigue Overload
walkthrough.text: Chance wants to make up for the earlier positioning error. The rogue uses a powerful skill that costs two fatigue to deal massive damage to the remaining goblin. Then Chance suffers one more fatigue to gain extra movement to reach a better position. That is three fatigue tokens in one turn. Chance now has heavy damage from the goblin attack in round one plus three fatigue. Combined, these are dangerously close to Chance's health limit. One more hit could knock Chance out.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Do Not Overuse Fatigue Early
walkthrough.text: Fatigue is a powerful resource, but it comes directly out of your health buffer. Using three fatigue in a single turn when you are already wounded is reckless. Chance is now one enemy attack away from being knocked out, which would force Brynn or Galaden to spend an action reviving instead of fighting or progressing. The rule of thumb is never let your damage plus fatigue exceed two-thirds of your health total. Save fatigue for critical moments — reaching a quest objective, finishing a boss, or escaping a deadly situation. Do not blow it on regular enemies.

walkthrough.title: The Third Mistake: Ignoring Weapon Flips
walkthrough.text: Galaden has been using the same side of his bow for the entire quest. His active weapon side deals good single-target damage with a Pierce surge. But the other side of his bow has an area-of-effect ability that would have been perfect when two goblins were standing next to each other on the platform. Galaden could have hit both enemies with one attack instead of picking them off one at a time. He never even checked what was on the other side of his weapon card.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Check Both Weapon Sides
walkthrough.text: Every weapon in Descent is double-sided for a reason. Each side offers a different tactical option — single-target versus area damage, melee versus ranged, offensive surges versus defensive surges. If you only use one side of your weapons, you are playing with half your toolkit. At the start of each quest, read both sides of every weapon card. Before combat, think about which mode suits the enemies you are about to face. Flipping your weapon at the right moment can turn a losing fight into a winning one.

walkthrough.title: Darkness Phase Round 2
walkthrough.text: The Darkness Phase hits again. The remaining goblin attacks Chance — and with damage plus fatigue already sky-high, the hit knocks Chance out. The rogue collapses on the dungeon floor. The threat timer advances again. Now Brynn or Galaden will have to spend one of their three precious actions next round to revive Chance. This is exactly the cascading problem that fatigue overuse creates.

walkthrough.title: Round 3: Revival and Recovery
walkthrough.text: Brynn moves adjacent to Chance and spends her first action to revive the rogue. Chance gets back up with partial health and all fatigue cleared. That revival cost Brynn one of her three actions this round — she can only take two more actions instead of three. Galaden uses his turn to finish off the last goblin and then interacts with a nearby quest token, advancing the objective. The team is back on track, but they lost tempo.

walkthrough.title: Chance Recovers and Goes Back
walkthrough.text: Now that Chance is revived and the area is clear, the rogue makes a smart play. Chance moves back toward the dungeon entrance to grab that search token from the first room. It turns out to contain a health potion — exactly what the team needed. If Chance had grabbed this token in the first round instead of rushing ahead, the rogue would have had the potion available before getting knocked out. Lesson reinforced — do not skip exploration tokens.

walkthrough.title: Galaden Flips His Weapon
walkthrough.text: Galaden finally checks the other side of his bow. The alternative mode has a Stun surge — spending a surge symbol applies the stun condition to the target, causing it to lose an action on its next activation. This would have been incredibly useful against the goblins earlier. Galaden flips his weapon to the stun side, preparing for whatever enemies the next exploration reveals. From now on, Galaden will think about which weapon mode to use before each combat encounter.

walkthrough.title: Exploring Further
walkthrough.text: The team pushes forward together this time — no one rushing ahead alone. Brynn explores a new section, revealing a larger chamber with a mini-boss enemy and more search tokens. But instead of charging in, the group pauses. They check their fatigue levels, consider their weapon modes, and plan their approach. Brynn will tank the mini-boss, Galaden will apply stun from range, and Chance will circle around for a flanking bonus. This is how a coordinated team operates.

walkthrough.title: The Coordinated Attack
walkthrough.text: The plan executes beautifully. Galaden shoots first and lands a stun on the mini-boss — it will lose an action during the Darkness Phase. Brynn moves in and attacks with her sword, dealing solid damage. Chance flanks from the side and uses a skill card for bonus damage on the attack. Three heroes, three actions each, all focused and coordinated. The mini-boss takes massive damage in a single round. Compare this to the chaotic first round where everyone acted independently.

walkthrough.title: The Threat Timer Pressure
walkthrough.text: The threat bar has been climbing each round. The app warns that the next threat increase will spawn reinforcement enemies. The team realizes they need to push toward the quest objective now rather than clearing every enemy. Sometimes the right play is to bypass enemies and rush the objective. Use your remaining actions efficiently — move toward the goal, interact with the objective token, and finish the quest before the threat overwhelms you.

walkthrough.title: Completing the Quest
walkthrough.text: Brynn holds the mini-boss at bay while Galaden and Chance rush to the quest objective. Chance interacts with the objective token, and the app resolves the quest conclusion. The team succeeds — barely. The threat was one step away from spawning a wave of reinforcements that would have overwhelmed them. Every wasted action — the missed search token, the fatigue overuse, the knockout and revival — nearly cost them the quest. Tight margins are normal in Descent.

walkthrough.title: Post-Quest Crafting
walkthrough.text: After the quest, the app takes you to the hub town. The team reviews their collected materials and decides to craft a new weapon for Chance — one with a defensive surge ability to help the rogue survive better. They also upgrade one of Galaden's skills. Crafting is a group decision, so talk about who needs upgrades most. A stronger team benefits everyone in this cooperative campaign.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations and Lessons Learned
walkthrough.text: Congratulations — you have completed your first practice quest in Descent: Legends of the Dark. You saw three common mistakes in action. Chance rushed past exploration tokens and missed a critical health potion. Chance burned through fatigue recklessly and got knocked out. And Galaden ignored half his weapon by never flipping it. Remember: always grab exploration tokens, manage your fatigue carefully, and use both sides of your weapons. You are ready for the real campaign.

Demonstrates core actions, Darkness Phase, and common mistakes
Follow along even if playing different heroes

summary.title: Round 3: Revival and Recovery

Crafting uses materials collected during quests

bullets: Three mistakes: skipping exploration tokens, fatigue overuse, ignoring weapon flips

walkthrough.title: Communication Is Everything
walkthrough.text: Descent is a cooperative game, and your most powerful ability is talking to each other. Before anyone takes their turn, discuss the plan as a group. Who should go first? Which enemies are the biggest threats? Who needs healing? Where should we explore next? A team that communicates well will outperform a team of individually skilled players who act in isolation. Share information about your weapon modes, your available skills, and your fatigue levels. No secrets at this table — you are all on the same side.

walkthrough.title: Manage Fatigue Like a Resource
walkthrough.text: Fatigue is not just a penalty — it is a resource you spend for powerful effects. The key is treating it like a limited budget. Before spending fatigue, ask yourself three questions. Is this the most important moment to push my limits? Will I survive the upcoming Darkness Phase with this much fatigue? Could I achieve the same result without spending fatigue? Save your fatigue for game-changing plays — reaching a critical objective, landing a killing blow on a boss, or escaping a deadly trap. Burning fatigue on regular enemies in the early rounds leaves you vulnerable when it actually matters.

walkthrough.title: Flip Your Weapons Often
walkthrough.text: New players tend to find a weapon side they like and stick with it for the entire quest. This is a trap. Each weapon side is designed for different situations. One side might deal heavy single-target damage — great for bosses. The other side might have an area effect or apply a debilitating condition — great for groups of weaker enemies. Get in the habit of checking both sides before every combat encounter. The Ready Weapon action is never wasted if it means your next two or three attacks are significantly more effective. Think of weapon flipping as your tactical advantage.

walkthrough.title: Explore Thoroughly Before Moving On
walkthrough.text: When the app reveals new map sections, resist the urge to rush toward the quest objective. Take time to check every search token and interaction point. The items you find — healing potions, crafting materials, equipment — accumulate over the campaign and make your heroes significantly stronger. A missed potion in quest three might mean a knockout in quest five that you could have avoided. Assign one hero to sweep each area for tokens while the others handle enemies. Exploration is just as important as combat.

walkthrough.title: Use Elevation to Your Advantage
walkthrough.text: The 3D terrain is not just for show. Higher ground gives meaningful combat benefits in Descent. Whenever possible, position your ranged attackers on elevated platforms where they can fire down on enemies. Use staircases strategically — you can sometimes block enemy movement by controlling access points. When enemies are on high ground and you are below, prioritize getting up there or pulling them down. Ignoring elevation is like ignoring half the battlefield. Read what the app says about elevation bonuses and plan your positioning accordingly.

walkthrough.title: Respect the Threat Timer
walkthrough.text: The threat timer is the silent enemy that many groups underestimate on their first few quests. It advances every round, and once it hits certain thresholds, bad things happen — reinforcements spawn, hazards appear, the situation gets worse. You cannot play Descent like a slow, careful dungeon crawl. You need to balance thoroughness with urgency. If the threat is climbing fast, it might be better to skip some enemies and push straight for the objective. If the threat is low, take your time to explore and collect items. Reading the threat level and adjusting your pace is one of the most important skills in the game.

walkthrough.title: Coordinate Your Hero Roles
walkthrough.text: Each hero excels at something different. Brynn tanks damage and protects allies. Galaden deals ranged damage and controls enemies with conditions. Chance strikes hard from flanking positions. Kehli supports with devices and utility. Syrus deals area damage with magic. Vaerix heals and buffs the team. Play to your hero's strengths and let your teammates cover your weaknesses. A guardian who tries to be a damage dealer and a rogue who tries to be a tank are both playing suboptimally. Know your role, trust your team, and focus on what your hero does best.

walkthrough.title: Plan Your Crafting Progression
walkthrough.text: Between quests, you will craft new weapons, armor, and items. Do not just craft whatever sounds cool — plan ahead. Look at the crafting options available and discuss as a group who needs upgrades most. A team where one hero has amazing gear and the others are still using starting weapons is weaker than a team where everyone has decent upgrades. Spread your resources. Also, read the weapon descriptions carefully — some crafted weapons unlock entirely new tactical options through their surge abilities and flip sides. Your crafting choices shape your team for the rest of the campaign.

summary.title: Communication Is Everything
bullets: Discuss turn order, threats, and plans before each round
Share weapon modes, skill availability, and fatigue levels openly
Coordinated teams outperform individuals acting alone
No secrets — you are all on the same side

summary.title: Manage Fatigue Like a Resource
bullets: Treat fatigue as a budget — spend it on game-changing plays only
Ask: is this critical? Will I survive the Darkness Phase? Is there a free alternative?
Save fatigue for objectives, boss kills, and escapes
Burning fatigue on regular enemies leaves you vulnerable later

summary.title: Flip Your Weapons Often
bullets: Each weapon side covers different tactical situations
Check both sides before every combat encounter
Ready Weapon action is never wasted if it improves your next attacks
Single-target vs area, offensive vs defensive — match the situation

summary.title: Explore Thoroughly Before Moving On
bullets: Search tokens contain potions, materials, and equipment
Items accumulate over the campaign — missed tokens hurt later
Assign one hero to sweep for tokens while others handle enemies
Exploration is just as important as combat

summary.title: Use Elevation to Your Advantage
bullets: Higher ground provides combat benefits
Position ranged attackers on elevated platforms
Control staircase access to funnel enemy movement
Prioritize gaining or neutralizing elevation advantages

summary.title: Respect the Threat Timer
bullets: Threat advances every round — bad things happen at thresholds
Balance thorough exploration with urgency to reach objectives
High threat: skip enemies, rush objective. Low threat: explore and collect
Reading threat level and adjusting pace is a critical skill

summary.title: Coordinate Your Hero Roles
bullets: Each hero has a role: tank, ranged DPS, melee striker, support, mage, healer
Play to your hero's strengths — let teammates cover weaknesses
A guardian tanking and a rogue striking is optimal teamwork
Know your role, trust your team, focus on what your hero does best

summary.title: Plan Your Crafting Progression
bullets: Discuss upgrades as a group — spread resources across the team
Even upgrades across all heroes beats one overpowered hero
Read crafted weapon descriptions: new surge abilities and flip sides
Crafting choices shape your team for the entire campaign

walkthrough.title: Action Economy Optimization
walkthrough.text: In Descent, every action matters because you only get three per hero per round. The key to advanced play is maximizing the value of each action. An attack that also applies a stun condition is worth more than a plain attack, because the stun effectively removes one of the enemy's actions during the Darkness Phase. A move that positions you for both an attack this turn and a flank next turn is worth more than moving to an immediate but dead-end position. Always think two turns ahead. What will the board look like after the Darkness Phase? Where will enemies move? Plan your actions to set up future turns, not just react to the current one.

walkthrough.title: Threat Timer Management
walkthrough.text: Advanced groups treat the threat timer as a strategic variable, not just a countdown clock. In the early rounds of a quest, the threat is low and you have time to explore, collect items, and position carefully. But every round spent not progressing toward the objective costs you. The optimal approach is to identify the minimum number of rounds needed to reach the objective and work backward from there. If you estimate the quest should take six rounds and you have eight rounds of threat tolerance, you have two rounds of breathing room for exploration and item collection. Spend those two rounds wisely, then shift to objective-focused play.

walkthrough.title: Target Priority and Focus Fire
walkthrough.text: Not all enemies are equal threats. Advanced groups identify the most dangerous enemy each round and focus fire to eliminate it before it acts. The priority order is generally: enemies with area attacks that can hit multiple heroes, ranged enemies in hard-to-reach positions, enemies about to attack a wounded hero, and then everything else. Splitting your damage across three enemies and leaving all three alive means all three attack during the Darkness Phase. Focusing all damage on one enemy and killing it means only two attack. Dead enemies deal zero damage — always reduce the enemy count as quickly as possible.

walkthrough.title: Fatigue Timing Windows
walkthrough.text: Expert players identify specific moments where spending fatigue yields maximum value. The best fatigue windows are: right before the quest objective is within reach, when one more action kills a boss before it gets another turn, and when you need to revive a downed ally in a critical position. The worst fatigue windows are: against regular enemies in early rounds, when the Darkness Phase is about to deal damage to you, and when you have no healing available. A single well-timed fatigue expenditure can save the entire quest. Three poorly-timed fatigue expenditures will lose it.

walkthrough.title: Weapon Flip Sequencing
walkthrough.text: Master-level play involves planning your weapon flips across multiple rounds, not just reacting to the current fight. Before each quest, look at the quest description and the types of enemies you expect to face. Set your weapons to the modes that will be most useful for the opening encounter. After clearing a room, flip your weapons to prepare for the next challenge before you explore — do not wait until you are already in combat with the wrong weapon side active. Some skill cards let you flip weapons as a free action. Build your skill loadout to include at least one flip ability so you can adapt mid-combat without spending an action.

walkthrough.title: Positioning and Zone Control
walkthrough.text: Advanced positioning goes beyond just staying near allies. Think about controlling chokepoints — narrow corridors where enemies must pass through to reach your squishier heroes. Place your guardian in the chokepoint and let enemies waste their movement trying to get past. Use ranged heroes to attack from behind the frontline without exposing themselves. When the map opens up into a large room, avoid clustering so area-of-effect enemy attacks cannot hit multiple heroes. Spread out enough to avoid area damage but close enough that you can revive fallen allies within one move action.

walkthrough.title: Campaign-Level Resource Planning
walkthrough.text: Descent is a sixteen-quest campaign, and advanced groups plan their resource allocation across the entire arc, not just quest by quest. Early quests are easier, so use them to explore thoroughly and collect maximum crafting materials. Invest early crafting resources into weapons over armor — damage output matters more than survivability in the early campaign because killing enemies faster reduces total damage taken. By mid-campaign, start investing in defensive upgrades and consumable items. Save your most powerful crafted items for the late-campaign quests where the difficulty spikes significantly.

walkthrough.title: Hero Synergy Combinations
walkthrough.text: Certain hero pairs create powerful synergies that are greater than the sum of their parts. Brynn and Galaden work together because Brynn holds enemies in place while Galaden fires from safety. Chance and Syrus combo well because Syrus can apply area conditions that Chance exploits for bonus damage on flanking strikes. Vaerix pairs with anyone because healing and buffing amplify any hero's effectiveness. When choosing heroes for a quest, think about which combinations will give you the most tactical flexibility. In a three-player game, pick heroes whose strengths cover each other's weaknesses rather than three heroes who all do the same thing.

walkthrough.title: Reading Enemy Patterns
walkthrough.text: After a few quests, you will start noticing that enemies follow predictable behavior patterns dictated by the app. Melee enemies always move toward the nearest hero. Ranged enemies prefer to stay at distance and shoot. Some enemies target the most wounded hero. Boss enemies often have special abilities that trigger at specific health thresholds. Once you recognize these patterns, you can manipulate enemy behavior. Position a high-defense hero closest to melee enemies to draw their aggression. Spread out against ranged enemies to minimize their effectiveness. And when fighting bosses, be ready for phase transitions when their health drops to certain levels.

walkthrough.title: Narrative Choices and Long-Term Impact
walkthrough.text: Descent's branching narrative means your story choices have real mechanical consequences. Some quest branches unlock better crafting materials or powerful unique items. Others affect which allies you have access to in later quests. When the app presents a narrative choice, think about the long-term implications, not just the immediate outcome. Choosing to help a village might mean you get reinforcements in a later quest. Choosing to chase the villain might mean you get a powerful artifact but lose an ally. There is no right answer, but informed choices lead to a more rewarding campaign. Discuss major decisions as a group — everyone is invested in the outcome.

summary.title: Action Economy Optimization
bullets: Maximize value per action — attacks with conditions > plain attacks
Moves that enable future plays > moves to dead-end positions
Think two turns ahead: what will the board look like after Darkness Phase?
Set up future turns, don't just react to the current situation

summary.title: Threat Timer Management
bullets: Estimate rounds needed for objective — subtract from threat tolerance
Remaining rounds = budget for exploration and item collection
Early rounds: explore and collect. Later rounds: objective-focused play
Treat threat as a strategic variable, not just a countdown

summary.title: Target Priority and Focus Fire
bullets: Priority: area attackers > ranged in cover > threats to wounded heroes > rest
Focus fire to kill one enemy rather than wound three
Dead enemies deal zero damage — reduce enemy count ASAP
Splitting damage leaves all enemies alive to attack in Darkness Phase

summary.title: Fatigue Timing Windows
bullets: Best: objective within reach, boss kill before its turn, critical revival
Worst: regular enemies early, before Darkness Phase damage, no healing available
One well-timed fatigue spend can save a quest
Three poorly-timed spends will lose it

summary.title: Weapon Flip Sequencing
summary.text: Plan weapon flips across rounds, not reactively.
bullets: Set weapon modes for expected enemies before exploring new areas
Flip between encounters, not during — avoid wasting mid-combat actions
Build skill loadout with at least one free flip ability for adaptability
Preview quest enemies and pre-set weapons accordingly

Spread out in open rooms to avoid area attacks hitting multiple heroes

summary.title: Reading Enemy Patterns

Ranged enemies prefer distance — spread out to reduce effectiveness

No right answers — but informed choices lead to better outcomes
Discuss major decisions as a group — everyone is invested

entries.definition: Human guardian hero. High health and defense, specializes in protecting allies and absorbing damage. Excels at holding chokepoints and drawing enemy attention away from squishier teammates.
Elven ranger hero. Excels at ranged combat with bow weapons. Can attack from a distance and apply conditions to enemies. Best positioned on elevated terrain behind the frontline.
Hyrrinx rogue hero. Specializes in stealth, flanking, and high single-target damage. Lower health pool but devastating when attacking from advantageous positions. Requires careful fatigue management.
Dwarven engineer hero. Builds and deploys mechanical devices that provide utility effects. Can create traps, support gadgets, and area-denial tools. A versatile hero that adapts to many situations.
Human elementalist hero. Wields elemental magic for area-of-effect damage and condition application. Powerful against groups of enemies but needs allies to protect him in melee range.

Hybrid sentinel hero. A support-focused hero who heals allies and provides defensive buffs. Pairs well with any team composition. Lower damage output but keeps the team alive through sustained quests.

An action that grants movement points equal to the hero's speed stat. Each space entered costs one movement point. Movement can be split around other actions within the same turn. Terrain and elevation may cost additional movement.

An action to deal damage to an enemy within weapon range. Roll your hero dice, count hits, spend surges on weapon abilities, and report results to the app. You cannot attack the same enemy twice in one turn.
An action used on spaces with interaction tokens shown by the app. Opens doors, finds loot, triggers story events, talks to NPCs, and advances quest objectives. Tap the token in the app to resolve.

An action used at the edge of revealed map tiles to push into unknown territory. The app reveals new tiles, 3D terrain, and potentially spawns enemies. Essential for quest progression but risky when done alone.

An action to activate a hero's skill card ability. Some skills are free reactions rather than full actions. Used skill cards are flipped or exhausted until refreshed. Skills define a hero's unique tactical options.

An action to flip one or both weapon cards to their other side, switching combat modes. Allows heroes to adapt their attacks to different tactical situations. Can also occur via certain skill effects or resting.
One side of a double-sided weapon card. Shows the weapon's stats, dice pool, range, and surge abilities for that combat mode. The face-up side is the active mode used when attacking.

The reverse side of a weapon card offering a different combat mode. May have different attack type (melee vs ranged), different surge abilities, or different damage potential. Flip to access this mode.

The act of turning a weapon card from one side to the other to change combat modes. Done via the Ready Weapon action, certain skills, or resting. A core tactical mechanic — each side offers different strengths for different situations.

Weapons created between quests using collected materials in the hub town. Crafted weapons are stronger than starting weapons with better stats, more powerful surge abilities, and more versatile flip sides. Crafting choices persist across the campaign.

A token placed on a hero card when using certain powerful abilities or gaining extra movement. Fatigue occupies the same health track as damage — if damage plus fatigue reaches health total, the hero is knocked out. Removed by resting, skills, or being revived.

The number of fatigue tokens a hero must take to activate a specific ability or gain extra movement. Listed on skill cards and certain weapon surge effects. Always weigh the benefit against the health risk before spending fatigue.

Methods to remove fatigue tokens from a hero card. Includes using specific skill cards, resting actions, being revived after knockout (clears all fatigue), and certain between-quest effects. Managing fatigue recovery is essential to sustained combat performance.

A card granting a hero a special ability unique to their class. Skills may cost an action, be free reactions, or trigger automatically. Used skills are exhausted and must be refreshed before reuse. Heroes gain new skills and upgrades throughout the campaign.

The process of returning an exhausted skill card to its ready state. Some skills refresh automatically at the start of the hero's next turn. Others require specific conditions, actions, or rest to refresh. Managing skill refresh timing is part of advanced play.

Custom dice unique to each hero that are rolled during attacks. Dice faces show hits (damage), surges (spend on weapon abilities), hit-plus-surge combinations, and blanks (misses). The number and color of dice rolled depend on the active weapon card.

Dice rolled when a hero is attacked to block incoming damage. Each shield symbol on the defense dice negates one point of damage. Defense dice are determined by the hero's defense stat and any equipped armor.
A die face symbol representing one point of damage dealt to the target. Multiple hits on a single roll are added together. The total hits minus the target's defense equals the actual damage dealt.

A die face symbol that can be spent to activate surge abilities on the active weapon card. Surges not spent on abilities are lost. Some weapon surge abilities cost one surge, others cost two. Surges are the source of most special combat effects.

Special effects listed on weapon cards that are activated by spending surge symbols rolled during an attack. Effects include bonus damage, piercing armor, applying conditions to enemies, and healing. Multiple surge abilities can be activated on a single attack if enough surges are rolled.

Physical plastic and cardboard terrain pieces that create a multi-level environment on the game map. Includes staircases, bookshelves, barricades, trees, barrels, and elevation platforms. Terrain affects movement costs, line of sight, and combat advantages.

The vertical positioning of figures on the map. Figures on higher terrain gain combat advantages. Moving between elevation levels requires stairs or ladders and may cost extra movement points. Ranged attacks from high ground are more effective.

The ability to see and target another figure for ranged attacks. Some 3D terrain pieces block line of sight, preventing ranged attacks through them. Positioning behind terrain can provide cover from enemy ranged attackers.

A token placed on the map by the app representing undiscovered content. Search tokens yield items and equipment. Interaction tokens mark quest objectives and story elements. Some tokens are hidden until heroes move adjacent. Always check for tokens before leaving an area.

The progressive map reveal system in Descent. The map starts small and expands as heroes explore. New tiles, terrain, and enemies appear only when revealed by the Explore action. Creates tension and unpredictability — you never know what lies ahead.

A specific type of exploration token that yields items when a hero interacts with it. Items found include healing potions, crafting materials, equipment, and consumables. Missing search tokens means missing potential upgrades that persist across the campaign.

The second phase of each round, occurring after all heroes have taken their turns. The app activates all enemies, advances the threat timer, and may trigger story events or spawn reinforcements. Heroes have no actions during this phase.

The first phase of each round where each hero takes a complete turn with three actions. Turn order is flexible and decided cooperatively by the team. All heroes complete their turns before the Darkness Phase begins.

During the Darkness Phase, the app activates enemies one group at a time, specifying their movement and attacks. Different enemy types follow different AI behavior patterns — melee rush nearest hero, ranged stay at distance, bosses use special abilities.

Various categories of enemies with different stats, behaviors, and abilities. Includes bandits, undead, goblins, monsters, and boss enemies. Each type has predictable AI patterns that advanced players can learn to manipulate and counter.

A bar in the app that advances each round during the Darkness Phase. As it increases, the quest becomes harder — stronger enemies spawn, hazards appear, and difficulty ramps. Reaching maximum threat may cause quest failure. Creates urgency to complete objectives quickly.

A between-quest activity in the hub town where heroes create new weapons and armor from collected materials. Crafted gear is stronger than starting equipment and persists across the campaign. Crafting is a group decision — discuss resource allocation as a team.

The between-quest location where heroes rest, craft equipment, purchase upgrades, and advance the story. The app manages the hub town interface. Decisions made here affect the entire team's capabilities for upcoming quests.

The system by which hero progress carries forward between quests. Weapons, skills, items, and story choices all persist. Heroes grow stronger over the sixteen-quest campaign. Failed quests do not end the campaign but may affect story outcomes and difficulty.

The story system where player choices during quests affect which future quests are unlocked, which allies appear, and how the campaign unfolds. Different groups may experience different story paths based on their decisions. Choices have real mechanical consequences.

A condition that deals damage over time. A poisoned figure takes additional damage at the start of their activation. Applied by certain enemy attacks, terrain hazards, or ability effects. Remove via specific skills or spending actions.

A condition that causes the affected figure to lose one action on their next turn. One of the most powerful conditions to apply to enemies — effectively reducing their threat. Applied by certain weapon surge abilities and hero skills.

A condition that reduces the effectiveness of healing received by the affected figure. Makes it harder to recover from damage. Particularly dangerous on heroes who rely on healing to sustain through long quests.
A condition that limits the movement options of the affected figure. A terrified hero may be unable to move toward certain enemies or areas. Restricts tactical flexibility and positioning options.

A condition that deals fire damage to the affected figure each round. Stacks with other damage sources and can quickly become lethal if not removed. Some terrain hazards also apply the burning condition.

The specific goal that must be completed to win a quest. Objectives include defeating boss enemies, rescuing NPCs, collecting items, reaching locations, or making story choices. The app tracks and displays current objectives throughout the quest.

The state when a hero's damage plus fatigue equals or exceeds their health total. The hero falls in their current space and cannot take actions. An adjacent ally can spend one action to revive them with partial health and cleared fatigue. A major tempo loss for the team.

The act of bringing a knocked-out hero back into play. An adjacent ally spends one action to revive the fallen hero, who recovers partial health and has all fatigue cleared. Costs the reviving hero one of their three precious actions for the round.
